title: Black Forest Labs Unveils First Model for Robotics in Shift to Physical AI
summary: Black Forest Labs, a German artificial intelligence research lab, is trialing its first model that can be used in robotics as the company expands beyond photorealistic image generation into the fast-growing field of physical AI.The Flux 3 model learns from images, video and audio and can be adapted to predict what should happen next based on what it sees, Black Forest Labs said in a statement on Thursday. title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 23
summary: Oil surged past $100 a barrel for the first time in two months after Iran-backed Houthi militants said they attacked two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea, escalating the Middle East conflict and threatening deeper supply disruptions.

title: Stocks Hit by AI and War Jitters as Oil Tops $100: Markets Wrap
summary: A surge in oil spurred by the escalating Iran war sent stocks and bonds lower, with Wall Street also rattled by renewed concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will pay off.

title: NYC’s Former HSBC Tower Seeks More Than $800 Million in Sale
summary: A Manhattan office tower where Amazon.com Inc. has offices is going up for sale.

title: Bund Yields Hit 15-Year High as $100 Oil Fans Inflation Concerns
summary: German bond yields rose to the highest level since 2011 as surging oil prices prompted bigger bets on interest-rate hikes, even as the European Central Bank kept its benchmark unchanged.

title: Nasdaq 100 Eyes Worst Day in Month as Oil Prices Soar Over $100
summary: The Nasdaq 100 Index tumbled, putting it on track for its worst day in a month, as doubts about the payoffs from debt-fueled artificial-intelligence spending boom dragged down big tech stocks. Oil prices pushed over $100 a barrel and bond yields rose as President Donald Trump threatened to escalate attacks in the Middle East.

title: Chocolate Maker Lindt Faces US Lawsuit Over Child Labor Allegations
summary: Lindt & Spruengli AG has been sued by an activist law firm in the US which alleges the Swiss chocolate maker is falsely promoting its efforts to battle child labor in Ghana and the Ivory Coast from where it sources cocoa.

title: How Leveraged Exchange-Traded Funds Are Suddenly Everywhere
summary: On this episode of Trillions, we discuss what’s fueling the boom and how these exchange-traded funds work in today’s market.

title: Russia’s Black Sea Waters Not Safe for Ships Amid Attacks
summary: Moscow warned that it is unsafe to navigate in its Black Sea waters after Ukraine has been ramping up attacks on Russia-linked ships in the area.

title: The Worrying Signal Embedded in the Momentum Selloff
summary: A loss of market leadership.

title: Ireland Says Alumina Probe Finds No Proof of Russia Weapons Link
summary: An investigation into alumina exports from Ireland to Russia has not found conclusive evidence linking a production plant to the manufacture of weapons in Russia, Irish Prime Minister Micheal Martin said.

title: Gold Falls as Middle East Conflict Reverses Dip-Buying Momentum
summary: Gold fell as escalating conflict in the Middle East continued to push up energy prices, fueling bets the Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

title: Blackstone Leads $400 Million Private Loan For HVAC Firm Buyout
summary: Blackstone Inc. led a roughly $400 million deal to support the buyout of Integra Testing Services by Harvest Partners LP, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Banks Start Trading Part of $35 Billion Chip Funding Package
summary: Wall Street banks have started buying and selling an initial piece of a $35 billion financing package for Broadcom Inc. and Anthropic PBC’s AI infrastructure expansion, with the biggest private credit deal ever now being offered to a wider pool of investors.

title: Teck Second-Quarter Earnings Triple as Copper Prices Advance
summary: Canadian miner Teck Resources Ltd. reported a tripling of second-quarter earnings on higher copper prices, while reaffirming output guidance, including at its flagship Quebrada Blanca mine in Chile.

title: Dollar Jumps on Haven Flows as Middle East War Intensifies
summary: The dollar got a boost Thursday from haven flows as the war in the Middle East intensified, pushing oil prices above $100 a barrel.

title: Dow Says Iran War Could Boost Guidance as Supply Chain Shielded
summary: Dow Inc. said that persistent tensions in the Middle East could help raise prices for plastics and packaging without lifting its material costs, padding profits and offering upside to the group’s third-quarter guidance.

title: Afrexim, AFC Invest in Dangote Refinery’s $2.5 Billion Placement
summary: Africa Finance Corp. and Africa Export-Import Bank are among investors that helped Dangote Petroleum Refinery & Petrochemicals FZE raise $2.5 billion in a private share placement as the Nigerian firm prepares for the continent’s biggest initial public offering.

title: US Treasury Yields Rise to 2026 Highs With Oil Sparking Fed Bets
summary: US Treasury yields rose to their highest levels of the year as the threat of escalation in the Iran war sent oil prices higher and boosted bets that the Federal Reserve could raise interest rates as soon as next week.

title: Mexican Mayor Shot Dead in his Office After Surviving Earlier Hit
summary: Armed assailants gunned down the mayor of a small Mexican city just south of the country’s capital in his office, at least the 14th sitting city leader to be killed since President Claudia Sheinbaum took office in late 2024.

title: TotalEnergies Profit Jumps 68% as Wars Drive Refining Boom
summary: TotalEnergies SE said second-quarter earnings surged as conflict-related supply disruptions boosted prices for crude and refined products, offsetting a drop in profit from its gas business.
